description: AppShell.svelte emits a <main> element (line 95) when state === 'Configured', and +page.svelte emits its own <main class='editor-main'> (line 98) inside the AppShell slot. This produces a nested <main> within <main> in the final DOM, violating the HTML5 spec which states the <main> element must not be nested (a document must not have more than one visible <main> element). REQ-FEED-023 specifies 'EditorPane is wrapped in a <main> with class editor-main' — but the spec did not account for the AppShell already supplying a <main>. The +page.svelte content is placed in AppShell's <slot /> which is itself inside <main> at AppShell.svelte:109. The PROP-FEED-S2-005 DOM test does not mount AppShell; it only tests a manually constructed DOM scaffold, so this nested-main issue is never caught by the test suite.
crossReference: promptnotes/src/routes/__tests__/main-route.dom.vitest.ts:82-111 — test does not mount AppShell, tests manually constructed DOM only
